title: Therapy room introduction
goal: Establish the warm minimalist office, the panda's guarded heaviness, and the calm distance between patient and psychologist before the dialogue begins.

title: Stillness settles into the room
goal: Hold the same wide-room composition with a subtle sense of emotional weight landing, preserving the therapy setting and relational geometry.

title: Panda begins to open up
goal: Move into a medium shot on the panda as she starts explaining her pain, with vulnerability readable before the full line lands.

title: Panda finishes the confession
goal: End on the panda carrying the emotional truth of the line, sincere rather than punchline-driven, with restrained body language.

title: Psychologist listens
goal: Show the psychologist receiving the panda's words with grounded attentiveness and open posture before he responds.

title: Gentle question lands
goal: Capture the psychologist asking, 'And which one do you like better?' with therapeutic calm and curiosity, not cleverness.

title: Panda caught by the question
goal: Return to a tighter shot on the panda as the question lands and her defensive framing begins to fall away.

title: Honest answer
goal: End on the panda admitting 'Neither' with exposed honesty and the smallest visible crack in her self-protection.

title: Shared frame of care
goal: Reunite the panda and psychologist in an intimate shared composition that feels steady, compassionate, and emotionally present.

title: Quiet beginning of healing
goal: Land the line 'Then that's where we start' and hold the panda absorbing it in silence, ending on warmth, steadiness, and a first hint of relief.
